title: [InstCombine] Fold (x + y) & (2^C) -> x & 2^C when y % 2^(C+1) == 0
summary:
The developers generalized a specific `and` optimization pattern into a reusable transformation by identifying the underlying mathematical property: `(x + y) & (2^C) → x & 2^C` when `y` is a multiple of `2^(C+1)`. Originally, the test case used `vscale` and constants (`shl 6`, `add -1`) that satisfied this condition implicitly. The patch extracts the core logic—checking if `y`'s known zero bits guarantee divisibility by `2^(C+1)`—using `KnownBits` analysis to handle arbitrary values.

This approach moves from a hardcoded pattern (e.g., `vscale` shifts) to a generic rule applicable to any `add` followed by `and` with a power-of-two mask. The generalization enables the optimization to fire in diverse contexts, such as loop induction variables (`trunciv`) and `urem` simplifications, without special-case code. The key lesson: isolate the essential preconditions (bitmask divisibility) and use LLVM's existing analysis (`KnownBits`) to verify them broadly.
